body.title: [sprint] Skill: /recover — rewind safeguard message
body.description:
Create a tiny skill `/recover` that outputs the proven rewind safeguard message, callable after a rewind so CC has an explicit command to emit and the user knows orientation was checked. The message: 'Rewound. Read today's slate and git log --oneline -10, reconcile against them, and trust slate+git over your memory before doing anything.' Verified to work perfectly during the rewind test as a cognitive reset signal.

**Affected files:** skills/recover/ (new skill directory + run script)

**Design rules:** Skills live in repo root skills/, not ~/.claude/skills/ (latter is NOT source of truth). No dependencies, no state. Idempotent: can be called multiple times.

**Scope boundary:** IN: the skill command, message output, registration. OUT: changes to rewind infrastructure itself (/context-load, /autocompact, Rewound? header behavior).

**Test plan:** Invoke `/recover`, verify output matches the safeguard message exactly. Verify callable multiple times without side effects or state mutation.
